case_title: MARC LUMER & COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. EAGLE ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A NEVADA et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT MARC LUMER & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE BEEN AWARE OF ITS CLAIM WHEN THE SEC SUSPENDED ITS ABILITY TO TRADE ON FEBRUARY 27, 2006. THE CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, CCP 339(1). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR #11055

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT MARC LUMER & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - off calendar. Insufficient notice. Moving party neglected to add five days for service by mail. CCP 1005(b). (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2008 Assigned To Arbitration On Dec-08-08

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2008 Continued To Nov-07-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gregory Rougeau of Manasian & Rougeau (291.8425) for cross defendant and moving party
Blair White (209.736.9661) for cross complainant and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT MARC LUMER & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained with 30 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff should have been aware of its claim when the SEC suspended its ability to trade on February 27, 2006. The claim is barred by the two year statute of limitations, CCP 339(1). Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Patrick Heard, CSR #11055
